html:
Exclusive to:  
This content is available only after activating the Shadow Rift

Dark Tatters are a crafting item within both the Hamlet DLC and Don't Starve Together. Dark Tatters are used for crafting Void Gear at the Shadowcraft Plinth. They are obtained by defeating Ink Blights that spawn from Shadow Rifts.

Trivia

  • Its name may be a reference to Dark Matters.
  • Prior to the Terrors Below update to DST, Dark Tatters were only found in Hamlet, making them one of few items that has been transposed into DST from the DLC.
    • Dark Tatters in Hamlet compared to DST have a large cosmetic difference, with the Hamlet tatters being more akin to black, bat-shaped pieces of cloth, whereas the tatters found in DST are red, making them appear as the red cowls the Ancient Herald and Inkblights wear.

Gallery

  • Old version of dropped Dark Tatter in DST

“All torn up.”

“All that's left of that poor shadow monster.”
–Walter
“Tatters of spacetime.”
–Wanda
Dark Tatters are a crafting item within both the Hamlet DLC and Don't Starve Together. Dark Tatters are used for crafting Void Gear at the Shadowcraft Plinth. They are obtained by defeating Ink Blights that spawn from Shadow Rifts.

Its name may be a reference to Dark Matters.
Prior to the Terrors Below update to DST, Dark Tatters were only found in Hamlet, making them one of few items that has been transposed into DST from the DLC.
Dark Tatters in Hamlet compared to DST have a large cosmetic difference, with the Hamlet tatters being more akin to black, bat-shaped pieces of cloth, whereas the tatters found in DST are red, making them appear as the red cowls the Ancient Herald and Inkblights wear.

'''Dark Tatters''' are a crafting item within both the ''[[Hamlet]]'' DLC and ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. Dark Tatters are used for crafting Void Gear at the [[Shadowcraft Plinth]]. They are obtained by defeating [[Ink Blight]]s that spawn from [[Shadow Rift]]s.

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Its name may be a reference to [[Wikipedia:Dark Matters|Dark Matters]].
* Prior to the [[Terrors Below]] update to DST, Dark Tatters were only found in Hamlet, making them one of few items that has been transposed into DST from the DLC.
** Dark Tatters in Hamlet compared to DST have a large cosmetic difference, with the Hamlet tatters being more akin to black, bat-shaped pieces of cloth, whereas the tatters found in DST are red, making them appear as the red cowls the Ancient Herald and Inkblights wear.
